listitem.para: ANSI-capable terminal (using the logger::init() function will set up the terminal for colors or disable them if the terminal does not support colors)

sect2.title: Option 1: Install system-wide with Cmake
sect2.para:
ghrepocloneBroDudeGuyMan/console_logger cdconsole_logger mkdirbuild&&cdbuild cmake.. cmake--build. sudocmake--install. This installs:
headers to /usr/local/include
the library to /usr/local/lib
CMake config files to /usr/local/lib/cmake/console_logger

sect2.title: Option 2: Use as a subproject.
sect2.para: This helps larger projects track dependencies. Add it to your project: gitsubmoduleaddhttps://github.con/BroDudeGuyMan/console_logger.git Then in CMakeLists.txt add_subdirectory(external/console_logger) target_Link_libraries(<your_target>console_logger::console_logger) This way, there is no install step.

listitem.para: CMake support enabled (should be on by default for VS)

sect3.title: Option 1: Use as a subproject
sect3.para: This avoids install paths and permissions. It also helps with dependencies. MyProject/ ├──CMakeLists.txt ├──src/ └──external/ └──console_logger/ add_subdirectory(external/console_logger) target_link_libraries(<executable_name>PRIVATEconsole_logger::console_logger) Open the project folder in VS and it should configure and build automatically.

sect3.title: Option 2: Install system-wide
sect3.para: From a Developer Command Prompt for Visual Studio: gitclonehttps://github.com/<your-username>/console_logger.git cdconsole_logger mkdirbuild cdbuild cmake..-DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release cmake--build.--configRelease cmake--install. Then in your project: find_package(console_loggerREQUIRED) target_link_libraries(<executable_name>PRIVATEconsole_logger::console_logger)

The logger by default will use colors, and Linux machines should have no issues with colors. Windows command prompts may have issues with ANSI codes, so using logger::init() may be useful. It will check whether the current Windows command prompt has support for colors, and if not will disable them.

sect1.title: Usage with CMake (<tt>find_package</tt>)
sect1.para: After installation: find_package(console_loggerREQUIRED) target_link_libraries(<your_target>PRIVATEconsole_logger::console_logger) You do not need to manually add include directories.

sect1.title: Usage in Code

listitem.para: Colors are enabled by default on Linux and MacOS

Windows has patchy support
logger::init() automatically detects color support for terminals and is safe to call on all platforms.
Colors are autamatically disabled if support is not detected
Colors remain on if supported by the console
Never prints raw ANSI codes when color is disabled
Never fails if colors are not supported
Never requires global objects or macros
Safe for projects of any kind: other libraries, tools, and small utilities
